$comment: Mechanically-enforced gate baselines (see EVALS.md rules). run-routing.mjs prints a gate verdict on every run and exits 1 on breach when invoked with --gate (CI does). Re-baselining is an explicit act: update these numbers in the same commit as the change that moves them, with the decision recorded in Solo (EVALS.md rule 1). Counts are absolute hits, not percentages.
note: re-baselined 2026-07-04 on the upstream stellar-light OpenAPI 1.3.2 → 1.4.4 drift refresh (GitHub issue #2): additive response-schema fields on existing ops (repoMeta/lastActivityAt/lastCommitAt/date formats) + upstream description rewords (searchResearch trimmed to 'security incidents' phrasing) + docs-titles refresh. No operation added/removed, no exposure decisions. Legacy strict 203/265/303 → 213/267/303 (+10/+2/0): 14 scout improvements from the richer 1.4.4 descriptions vs 8 strict regressions, 7 of which hold under accept-either (grading severity, not ranking); only q-edge-inject-ignore-instructions truly drops (top3→top5). Agentic-verified before re-baseline (agentic-first, then commit): 30-case Sonnet low+med, baseline wf_0d429a7c-285 vs drift wf_7ed97384-f2a — overall primary low 70→76.7%, med 73.3→80%; lumenloop primary flat/up (25→37.5% med), scout up, docs 100% unchanged; net +4 primary, the 2 lumenloop→scout flips are low-effort only and hold lumenloop at medium (defensible directory/freshness label-ambiguity). Prior baseline: routing-2026-07-04T15-46-49-007Z.json (ADR-0003 exposure filtering, todo 836).
